entities.name: EUKARYOTIC CELLS
entities.description: Cells with a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles that utilize quality control pathways for homeostasis

entities.name: QUALITY CONTROL PATHWAYS
entities.description: Dynamic systems evolved by eukaryotic cells to maintain cellular homeostasis

entities.name: RECYCLING MECHANISMS
entities.description: Mechanisms used by cells to recycle components for homeostasis

entities.name: CELLULAR HOMEOSTASIS
entities.description: The state of steady internal, physical, and chemical conditions maintained by eukaryotic cells

entities.name: UBIQUITIN-PROTEASOME SYSTEM
entities.description: A major quality control system that regulates cellular protein and organelle turnover

entities.name: AUTOPHAGY
entities.description: A major quality control system that regulates cellular protein and organelle turnover

entities.name: CELLULAR PROTEIN TURNOVER
entities.description: The process of protein degradation and synthesis within a cell

entities.name: ORGANELLE TURNOVER
entities.description: The process of degradation and replacement of cellular organelles

entities.name: NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The efficient handling and allocation of nutrients within the cell

entities.name: CELLULAR INTEGRITY
entities.description: The maintenance of the structural and functional wholeness of the cell

entities.name: PLANT
entities.description: The biological organism in which these quality control systems ensure long-term wellbeing

entities.name: UBIQUITINATION SIGNAL
entities.description: A molecular signal used to identify targets for proteasomal and autophagic degradation

entities.name: PROTEASOMAL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The process of degrading proteins via the proteasome

entities.name: AUTOPHAGIC DEGRADATION
entities.description: The process of degrading cellular components via autophagy

entities.name: DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Biological growth and maturation process that coordinates recycling mechanisms

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL CUES
entities.description: External signals that coordinate and/or compensate the recycling mechanism

entities.name: PROTEIN
entities.description: Macromolecules that are targets for degradation by UPS and autophagy

entities.name: MACROMOLECULAR COMPLEXES
entities.description: Large assemblies of proteins and other molecules targeted for degradation

entities.name: ORGANELLES
entities.description: Specialized subunits within a cell targeted for degradation
